title: Singapore Imposes First SAF Levy as Southeast Asia Expands Production
content: Singapore has introduced a new levy on air tickets, ranging from $0.75 to $32, to fund the adoption of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). This initiative aims to slash emissions using cleaner burning fuel derived from sources like used cooking oil, without requiring aircraft modifications. The move reinforces Southeast Asia's position as a burgeoning global SAF hub, especially as the U.S. faces potential policy shifts. Singapore already hosts the region's largest plant and is breaking ground on a next-generation facility by Aether Fuels this year. Regional momentum is building: Thailand launched a SAF plant in 2025, while Malaysia and Vietnam hit production milestones. Indonesia has also announced expansion plans. The levy supports the transition to greener aviation while capitalizing on the region's growing production capacity.
author: Analysts Account
